elements.description: Two separate stair-detail callout tags are printed near this same physical staircase: 'ST2 A-20 ดูแบบขยายบันได' (printed near ซักล้าง, upper-left of the stairwell) and 'ST3 A-21 ดูแบบขยายบันได' (printed near ทางเดิน, lower-left of the stairwell). NOT resolved here whether these are 2 distinct callouts for the same single staircase (e.g. upper flight vs lower flight) or genuinely 2 separate physical stairs stacked close together in plan — flagged for human review since both tags point at essentially the same drawn stair icon.

elements.description: Callout reading 'ผนัง คสล.' (concrete wall) with tag 'ST3 A-19', positioned right beside a separate 'ม่านั่ง คสล. ดูแบบขยาย' (concrete bench, see expanded detail) callout at the bottom-right corner near เฉลียง -- appears to be a second, distinct detail bundled onto the same A-19 sheet as ST1's own threshold-step detail (A-19 likely contains both ST1's steps AND this small concrete wall/bench feature), not a duplicate or conflicting stair mark for the same object as บันได_ST1.

elements.description: Circled numeral callouts (door number) and boxed numeral callouts (window number) are printed at each room per the symbol legend (หน้า03), plus several small triangle-shaped vent/fixture symbols with numbers that are not part of the door/window legend. The bracket number in each room's box is a floor-material category (matches the A-01 material list พื้น items 1-5), the trailing number is the door/window opening reference. Whether each trailing tag is a circle (door) or box (window) shape is not reliably distinguishable at this resolution for every room.

warnings: This is the ground-floor plan sheet (A-04, แปลนพื้นชั้น 1) of a 2-story house — the roof plan is on its own separate sheet (A-06, หน้า08), and the 2nd floor is on its own separate sheet (A-05, หน้า07, see sibling _หน้า07_floor_plan_upper.json).

This house has THREE distinct stair-related callouts printed on the ground floor (ST1/A-19 at the เฉลียง threshold step, and a dual ST2/A-20 + ST3/A-21 callout both pointing at the single main staircase icon in โถง) plus a possible 4th ambiguous callout near the east wall past เฉลียง — unlike house #3 which had only 2 stairs (ST.1 porch step, ST.2 main switchback). This house's A-01 index also printed 'แบบขยายบันได ST.3' for BOTH A-21 and A-22 (see _หน้า03_index.json), consistent with ST.3 being a 2-sheet detail (plan A-21 + section/hardware A-22, mirroring house #3's ST.2 pattern) — but which physical stair is 'ST2' vs 'ST3' on this floor plan is not conclusively resolved here; flagged for cross-check once A-19/A-20/A-21/A-22 (หน้า19-22) are read.

GRID-RELEVANT FOR หน้า00 MASTER (reporting only, master file not edited): top dimension chain reads left to right: 0.60 (west overhang, beyond grid1) | 5.00 (grid1-2, sub-split 2.30+2.70, further sub-split 1.10+1.20 within the 2.30 zone) | 3.50 (grid2-3) | 0.60 (east overhang, beyond grid3) — matches the master grid's x_lines (1=0.0, 2=5.0, 3=8.5) exactly, no dummy grid needed on the x-axis for this sheet. Left dimension chain reads top to bottom: 2.00 (north overhang zone, above gridE — this genuinely resolves part of the site plan's (หน้า05) previously-flagged undimensioned top setback zone) | 2.70 (gridE-D) | 2.30 (gridD-C) | 10.00 bracket total (E to A) | 3.50 (gridC-B) | 1.50 (gridB-A) | 1.60 (south, below gridA, before the hatched grading wedge) — matches the master grid's y_lines (E=0.0, D=2.7, C=5.0, B=8.5, A=10.0) exactly. Combined with the site plan's total lot depth of 16.20m, this floor plan's own drawn extent (2.00+10.00+1.60=13.60m) accounts for most but not all of the lot depth — the remaining ~2.60m is presumably further front/back yard beyond what this floor-plan sheet dimensions, consistent with the site plan showing extra road-frontage setback not repeated here.

The master grid's dummy row E' (-1.9m, from structural S-02/S-03 pile/footing plans) is NOT visible as a distinct dimensioned zone on this architecture floor plan — the 2.00m zone above gridE (ซักล้าง/ดินถม area) is a different, smaller offset than the structural E' dummy grid's 1.9m; not treated as confirming or contradicting the structural dummy grid, just noting they are not the same feature.

Individual door/window counts and the circle-vs-box (door-vs-window) glyph distinction per room, plus several small triangle vent/fixture symbols, were not confidently resolvable at this image resolution — flagged for zoom review rather than guessed.

Elevation-direction callout bubble (bottom of sheet: 3/A-09 top, 2/A-08 left, 4/A-10 right, 1/A-07 bottom) and section-cut callout arrows (tags '1/A-11' appearing twice, '2/A-12' appearing twice, '3/A-13' appearing twice, at various points around the plan perimeter) are printed on this sheet — read and cross-referenced when extracting those sheets, not re-itemized as elements here.

WC1 is detailed on its own dedicated 1:25 sheet (A-16, หน้า16) — this floor-plan-level box is superseded by that sheet for exact fixture layout; see _หน้า16_wc1_plan.json (to be written when that page is read). Unlike house #3 (WC.1+WC.2 sharing one detail sheet), this house has 3 separate WC detail sheets (A-16/A-17/A-18 for WC.1/WC.2/WC.3) per the index — only WC1 appears on this ground floor plan.
